title: Merge API result swallowed — UI buttons permanently locked on merge failure
description: At lines 1227–1238, `PrActionService.merge()` returns `ApiResult<Unit>` but the return value is never captured. The `scope.launch` block catches only `Exception` (which `ApiResult.Error` is not), then unconditionally disables all action buttons. When merge fails (network error, 409 stale version, auth failure, merge precondition violation, etc.) the user sees no error notification AND permanently loses the Merge/Approve/Decline/Needs-Work buttons for the current PR view. The only recovery is closing and reopening the PR tab.
suggestedFix: Capture the return value: `val result = PrActionService.getInstance(project).merge(...)`. Inside `invokeLater`, branch on `result`: on `ApiResult.Success` disable buttons (and optionally refresh); on `ApiResult.Error` show a notification via `WorkflowNotificationService.notifyError` and leave buttons enabled so the user can retry. Pattern mirrors the correct `declineButton` handler at lines 1263–1281.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed at lines 1227-1238. `PrActionService.merge()` returns `ApiResult<Unit>` but the return value is discarded. The `invokeLater` block unconditionally disables all four action buttons (merge/approve/needsWork/decline) regardless of whether the merge succeeded or failed. The `catch (e: Exception)` at line 1239 only intercepts thrown exceptions — `ApiResult.Error` is a return value, not a thrown exception, so API failures (409 stale-version, 401 auth, network error) silently fall through and leave the user with a fully-disabled button bar and no error message. Compare with `declineButton` at lines 1262-1278, which correctly captures the result and branches on `result.isError`. Downgraded from Critical to High: the 'permanently locked' claim is slightly overstated since refreshing or reopening the PR tab recovers the buttons, but the failure path is genuinely broken for the normal use case. Fix: capture the `ApiResult<Unit>` and mirror the `declineButton` pattern.
recommendedAction: Capture the return value of `merge()` and mirror the `declineButton` pattern at lines 1262-1278: on `ApiResult.Error`, call `WorkflowNotificationService.getInstance(project).notifyError(...)` and leave buttons enabled; on `ApiResult.Success`, disable them as today.

title: saveDescription result swallowed — UI shows saved description even on server failure
description: In `DescriptionSubPanel.saveDescription()` (lines 1975–1986), `PrActionService.updateDescription()` returns `ApiResult<Unit>` but the result is discarded. The `invokeLater` block always re-enables the Update button and calls `showDescription(newDescription)`, so the panel displays the unsaved text as though it was persisted. If the PUT returns a 409 stale-version conflict, network error, or auth failure, the user has no indication of failure and the description on the server remains unchanged.
suggestedFix: Capture the result and branch: on `ApiResult.Success` call `showDescription(newDescription)` as now; on `ApiResult.Error` re-show the original description (`showDescription(currentDescription)`) and surface the error message via `WorkflowNotificationService.notifyError`. Same pattern as `saveTitleEdit` (lines 1563–1578) which correctly reverts and shows a notification on failure.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed at lines 1975-1986. `PrActionService.updateDescription()` returns `ApiResult<Unit>` (verified at PrActionService.kt line 307) but `saveDescription()` discards the result. The `invokeLater` block unconditionally sets `currentDescription = newDescription` and calls `showDescription(newDescription)`, so the panel displays stale text as saved even when the PUT returns a 409 or network error. `PrActionService.updateDescription` goes through `modifyPullRequest` which already retries on 409, so true 409 failures are genuine server-side rejections. Compare with `saveTitleEdit` at lines 1563-1578 which correctly handles `ApiResult.Success` vs `ApiResult.Error` and reverts the title label on failure. Fix is safe: on error, revert to `currentDescription` (the pre-edit value, which is not yet overwritten) and show a notification.
recommendedAction: Capture the `ApiResult<Unit>` from `updateDescription()` inside the `scope.launch`. In `invokeLater`, on `ApiResult.Success` keep current behavior; on `ApiResult.Error` call `showDescription(currentDescription)` (revert) and show a notification via `WorkflowNotificationService`. Pattern mirrors `saveTitleEdit` at lines 1563-1578.

title: BitbucketServiceImpl.declinePullRequest bypasses PrActionService's 409-retry logic
description: The agent-facing `declinePullRequest` (lines 741–762) does a single GET then a bare `api.declinePullRequest(…, prDetail.version)` call. `PrActionService.decline()` (service/PrActionService.kt:196–243) has an explicit 409 detect-and-retry path with cache invalidation that was introduced as an audit fix. The agent tool path silently misses that retry, so a concurrent PR update between the GET and the POST will give the agent a spurious error rather than transparently retrying. `BitbucketServiceImpl.updatePrDescription` (lines 764–792) has the same gap: it calls `api.updatePullRequest` directly instead of `api.modifyPullRequest` (which owns the retry). This is inconsistent with the comment at line 291–308 in the same file that explicitly routes `addReviewer` through `PrActionService.addReviewer` (primary-repo case) or through `modifyPullRequestForAgent` (non-primary case) for exactly this reason.
suggestedFix: For `declinePullRequest`: mirror the `addReviewer` pattern. For primary-repo case, delegate to `PrActionService.getInstance(project).decline(prId)` and map the result to `ToolResult<Unit>`. For non-primary repo, add a private `declinePullRequestForAgent` helper analogous to `modifyPullRequestForAgent` that uses `BitbucketBranchClient.modifyPullRequest`-style retry. For `updatePrDescription`: route through `api.modifyPullRequest(RepoCoords(…), prId) { current -> updateDescriptionMutator(current, description) }` (the mutator already exists as a top-level function in PrActionService.kt) so the retry fires on 409.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: BitbucketServiceImpl.declinePullRequest (lines 741-762) performs a single GET then calls `api.declinePullRequest(...)` directly at line 754, bypassing PrActionService.decline()'s 409-detect-and-retry path (PrActionService.kt lines 196-243). Similarly, `updatePrDescription` (lines 764-792) calls `api.updatePullRequest` at line 785 directly instead of routing through `api.modifyPullRequest` with its retry-on-409. By contrast, `addReviewer` (lines 300-337) and `removeReviewer` (lines 852-881) both explicitly route through `modifyPullRequestForAgent` / `PrActionService` for exactly this reason (with detailed comments at lines 288-299). The inconsistency is real: a concurrent PR update between the agent's GET and its POST produces a spurious error rather than a transparent retry. This is the direct agent-facing path — a user asks the agent to decline or update a description and it fails under concurrent edits when the UI path would succeed.
recommendedAction: For declinePullRequest: delegate to PrActionService.getInstance(project).decline(prId) on the primary repo; add a private fetchAndDeclineForAgent helper for non-primary repos that mirrors the retry pattern. For updatePrDescription: replace the direct api.updatePullRequest call with api.modifyPullRequest using the updateDescriptionMutator already defined in PrActionService.kt.

title: BitbucketServiceImpl.updatePrDescription lacks 409 stale-version retry (agent path)
description: The agent-facing `updatePrDescription` (lines 764–793) performs a naive GET+PUT without 409-retry. The parallel methods `addReviewer` (line 300) and `updatePrTitle` (line 343) both check `isPrimaryRepo` and route to `PrActionService` (which uses `modifyPullRequest` with a built-in 409 retry) or `modifyPullRequestForAgent`. `updatePrDescription` was never updated to follow this routing pattern, so any concurrent edit between the GET and the PUT silently loses the agent's update with a 409 error.
suggestedFix: Apply the same single-repo/multi-repo routing pattern: for the primary repo, delegate to `PrActionService.updateDescription(prId, description)` which already uses `modifyPullRequest` with retry. For non-primary repos, call `modifyPullRequestForAgent(api, prId, projectKey, repoSlug, "update description") { current -> updateDescriptionMutator(current, description) }`. The `updateDescriptionMutator` pure function already exists in `PrActionService.kt`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed at lines 764-793 in BitbucketServiceImpl.kt. `updatePrDescription` does a bare GET+PUT with no 409-retry and no single-repo / multi-repo routing. `addReviewer` (line 300) and `updatePrTitle` (line 343) both check `isPrimaryRepo` and route: primary-repo → `PrActionService` (which uses `modifyPullRequest` with built-in 409 retry); non-primary → `modifyPullRequestForAgent` (also uses `modifyPullRequest`). `updatePrDescription` was never updated to follow this pattern: for the primary repo it bypasses `PrActionService.updateDescription` and does a raw GET+PUT, so it has no 409 protection; for non-primary repos it also does a raw GET+PUT. `updateDescriptionMutator` internal function already exists in `PrActionService.kt` (line 536) so the routing logic is straightforward to add. Medium severity is correct: this is an agent-path function, not triggered by typical UI interaction.
recommendedAction: Add the same `isPrimaryRepo` guard: for primary repo, delegate to `PrActionService.getInstance(project).updateDescription(prId, description).toToolResult(...)`. For non-primary repos, call `modifyPullRequestForAgent(api, prId, projectKey, repoSlug, "update description") { current -> updateDescriptionMutator(current, description) }`. The `updateDescriptionMutator` function is already internal in PrActionService.kt (line 536); make it internal-accessible or inline the equivalent lambda.

title: currentPr read on Dispatchers.IO without @Volatile — stale value visible on ARM
description: `currentPr` (line 100) is a plain `var` written on the EDT by `showPr()`, `showPrDetail()`, and `refreshCurrentPr()`, but read on `Dispatchers.IO` by two `scope.launch {}` blocks: (1) `renderPrHeader`'s coroutine at line 1427 (`val pr = currentPr`) which determines the approve/unapprove toggle label, and (2) `FilesSubPanel.openDiffViewer` at line 2581 (`val pr = currentPr ?: return@launch`) which reads `pr.toRef`/`pr.fromRef`. The JVM memory model does not guarantee visibility of non-volatile writes across threads. On ARM processors (macOS M1/M2/M3, which is the dev/CI environment) a CPU can serve a stale cached value, causing either wrong approve-button state or the diff viewer silently aborting on a false null read. `cachedUsername` at line 1456 correctly uses `@Volatile` but `currentPr` and `currentPrId` do not.
evidence:
private var currentPr: BitbucketPrDetail? = null  // line 100, no @Volatile
...
scope.launch {  // Dispatchers.IO
    val currentUsername = resolveCurrentUsername()
    val pr = currentPr  // line 1427: cross-thread read without visibility guarantee
suggestedFix: Add `@Volatile` to `currentPr` and `currentPrId`: `@Volatile private var currentPr: BitbucketPrDetail? = null` and `@Volatile private var currentPrId: Int? = null`. This is the same fix applied to `cachedUsername` (line 1456). No logic changes are needed; `@Volatile` establishes the happens-before edge between the EDT write and the IO read.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `currentPr` at PrDetailPanel.kt line 100 is `private var currentPr: BitbucketPrDetail? = null` with no `@Volatile`. It is written on the EDT by `showPr()`/`showPrDetail()`/`refreshCurrentPr()` and read on Dispatchers.IO at two sites: line 1427 (`val pr = currentPr` inside a `scope.launch` on Dispatchers.IO that determines approve-button label) and line 2581 (`val pr = currentPr ?: return@launch` in `FilesSubPanel.openDiffViewer`). `cachedUsername` at line 1456 already uses `@Volatile` with an explicit KDoc comment explaining the EDT↔IO visibility guarantee. Under the JVM memory model, reads on a non-volatile field from a different thread than the writer are not guaranteed to see the latest value; on ARM/JVM (macOS M-series) the processor's relaxed memory model makes this practically observable. The fix (`@Volatile` on `currentPr` and `currentPrId`) is minimal and correct with no behavioral side-effects.
recommendedAction: Add `@Volatile` to `currentPr` (line 100) and `currentPrId` (line 99): `@Volatile private var currentPrId: Int? = null` and `@Volatile private var currentPr: BitbucketPrDetail? = null`. Same pattern already applied to `cachedUsername` at line 1456.

title: PrDetailPanel.showCreateForm and submitCreatePr bypass the shared BitbucketBranchClientCache
description: Three sites in `PrDetailPanel` call `BitbucketBranchClient.fromConfiguredSettings()` directly (lines 528, 714, 1706) to construct ad-hoc clients, bypassing the `BitbucketBranchClientCache` that all PR services share. The cache exists specifically to (a) reuse HTTP connections and OkHttp thread pools across the entire PR module, and (b) guarantee that a (url, client) pair is always consistent under concurrent URL changes (see `BitbucketBranchClientCache` lines 29–43). Ad-hoc calls always spin up a new OkHttp client and thread pool on each use, and can produce a client built for an old URL if the settings change between the `ConnectionSettings.getInstance()` read inside `fromConfiguredSettings()` and the client's first real call. A fourth site in `resolveCurrentUsername` (line 1460) does the same. `CreatePrDialog.searchUsers` (line 683) falls back to `fromConfiguredSettings()` when `BitbucketBranchClient.forRepo` returns null, which is acceptable, but the create-PR submit path at line 1035 uses `BitbucketBranchClient.forRepo` correctly.
evidence:
528:            val client = BitbucketBranchClient.fromConfiguredSettings() ?: return@launch
714:            val client = BitbucketBranchClient.fromConfiguredSettings() ?: return@launch
1460:        val client = BitbucketBranchClient.fromConfiguredSettings() ?: return null
suggestedFix: Inject a `BitbucketBranchClientCache` instance into `PrDetailPanel` (constructed once in the class body, like every other PR service) and replace the three `fromConfiguredSettings()` call sites with `clientCache.get()`. For `resolveCurrentUsername` (line 1460) this also means the username-resolution client is always consistent with the project/repo used everywhere else in the panel.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: PrDetailPanel.kt calls `BitbucketBranchClient.fromConfiguredSettings()` at lines 528, 714, 1460, and 1706, bypassing the BitbucketBranchClientCache that all PR services use. The cache class (BitbucketBranchClientCache.kt lines 29-43) exists specifically to atomically enforce (url, client) consistency under concurrent URL changes. These ad-hoc calls each spin up a fresh OkHttp client and thread pool, and can produce a client built for a stale URL in the gap between the ConnectionSettings read and first use. PrDetailPanel has no `clientCache` field — the oversight is structural. The fix (adding a `private val clientCache = BitbucketBranchClientCache()` field and replacing the four call sites) is safe and follows the exact pattern used by all other PR services.
recommendedAction: Add `private val clientCache = BitbucketBranchClientCache()` to PrDetailPanel and replace all four `BitbucketBranchClient.fromConfiguredSettings()` call sites (lines 528, 714, 1460, 1706) with `clientCache.get()`.

title: BitbucketServiceImpl.mergePullRequest (agent path) does not use PrActionService.merge's retry-on-409
description: `BitbucketServiceImpl.mergePullRequest` (lines 709–739) performs a GET to obtain the current `version` then calls `api.mergePullRequest(…, prDetail.version, …)` directly. `PrActionService.merge` (PrActionService.kt:153–183) routes through `client.mergePullRequestWithRetry` which fetches the version internally and retries on a stale-version 409. The agent tool therefore silently misses the retry logic on concurrent merge attempts. The comment in `PrActionService` (lines 143–151) documents this as the deliberately correct path for the UI; the agent should use the same hardened path.
suggestedFix: For the primary-repo case, delegate to `PrActionService.getInstance(project).merge(prId, strategyId=strategy, deleteSourceBranch=deleteSourceBranch, commitMessage=commitMessage)` and convert its `ApiResult` to `ToolResult<Unit>` with the same `.toToolResult(…)` helper already used by `addReviewer`/`updatePrTitle`. For non-primary repos, introduce a `mergePullRequestForAgent` private helper that calls `client.mergePullRequestWithRetry(RepoCoords(projectKey, repoSlug), …)` directly.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: BitbucketServiceImpl.mergePullRequest (lines 709-739) performs a GET at line 720 to fetch the current version, then calls `api.mergePullRequest(...)` directly at line 728 — no retry on 409. PrActionService.merge (lines 153-183) routes through `client.mergePullRequestWithRetry` at line 165, which fetches the version internally and retries once on a stale-version 409. The pattern is identical to the gap in declinePullRequest (ARC-1). For the agent, a concurrent merge attempt or PR detail update between the GET and the POST produces a spurious error. This is the same architectural inconsistency as ARC-1 — merge and decline are the two most version-sensitive operations, and both bypass the retry logic in the agent path while the UI path benefits from it.
recommendedAction: For the primary repo case, delegate to PrActionService.getInstance(project).merge(prId, strategyId=strategy, deleteSourceBranch=deleteSourceBranch, commitMessage=commitMessage) and convert ApiResult to ToolResult. For non-primary repos, add a private mergePullRequestForAgent helper that calls client.mergePullRequestWithRetry with the resolved RepoCoords.

title: CommentsViewModel.reply: Long comment ID silently truncated to Int without overflow guard
description: At line 95, `reply(parentCommentId: Long, ...)` calls `service.replyToComment(parentCommentId = parentCommentId.toInt(), ...)`. Bitbucket comment IDs are stored as `Long` in the server API and can exceed `Int.MAX_VALUE` (2,147,483,647) on busy projects. The overflow produces a negative Int, which causes the reply to target the wrong comment ID or receive a 404/400 from Bitbucket. `ActivitySubPanel.buildReplyLink()` has an explicit SEC-24 guard for this exact case (lines 2397–2399) but `CommentsViewModel.reply` does not.
evidence:
suspend fun reply(parentCommentId: Long, text: String): Boolean {
    val result = service.replyToComment(
        ...
        parentCommentId = parentCommentId.toInt(),  // truncates silently
suggestedFix: Add the same guard as `ActivitySubPanel` before the conversion: `if (parentCommentId < Int.MIN_VALUE || parentCommentId > Int.MAX_VALUE) { lastError = "Comment ID $parentCommentId exceeds Int range"; fire(); return false }`. Alternatively, update `BitbucketService.replyToComment` to accept `Long` throughout and remove the conversion (the deeper architectural fix).
verdictReasoning: Confirmed at CommentsViewModel.kt line 95: `parentCommentId.toInt()` with no overflow guard. `ActivitySubPanel.buildReplyLink` at lines 2397-2399 has the explicit SEC-24 guard (`if (rawId < Int.MIN_VALUE || rawId > Int.MAX_VALUE) return ...`) before the same conversion. The interface chain (`BitbucketService.replyToComment`, `PrActionService.replyToComment`, `BitbucketBranchClient.replyToComment`) all take `Int`, so the type mismatch propagates through the entire stack. However, Bitbucket Server comment IDs are auto-increment integers that would require billions of comments to exceed `Int.MAX_VALUE`; in practice this overflow is exceedingly unlikely on any real Bitbucket Server instance. The inconsistency with the SEC-24 guard is real but the exploitable surface is theoretical. Downgraded from Medium to Low. The deeper architectural fix (changing the stack to `Long`) is out of scope, but adding the ViewModel guard is a safe incremental improvement.
recommendedAction: Add the same guard as ActivitySubPanel SEC-24 before the `toInt()` conversion at CommentsViewModel.kt line 95: `if (parentCommentId < Int.MIN_VALUE || parentCommentId > Int.MAX_VALUE) { lastError = "Comment ID $parentCommentId exceeds Int range"; fire(); return false }`. The full architectural fix (changing `replyToComment` parameter to `Long` throughout) is a larger refactor and can be tracked separately.

title: Dead inline Create PR form in PrDetailPanel (~300 lines of unreachable code)
description: The entire CARD_CREATE card — `showCreateForm()` (line 466), `buildCreatePanel()` (line 555), `submitCreatePr()` (line 682), `renderCreateReviewers()` (line 760), `showCreateReviewerPopup()` (line 792), and all the fields they depend on (`createSourceBranchLabel`, `createTargetBranchCombo`, `createTitleField`, `createDescriptionArea`, `createReviewersPanel`, `createAddReviewerLink`, `createButton`, `createBackLabel`, `selectedReviewerUsernames`, `selectedReviewerDisplayNames`, `createRepoConfig`) — is dead code. `showCreateForm()` is the only public entry point to this card and it has zero callers outside the file; the CLAUDE.md and code comments confirm the only live Create PR path is `CreatePrLauncherImpl → CreatePrDialog`.
suggestedFix: Delete: `showCreateForm`, `buildCreatePanel`, `submitCreatePr`, `renderCreateReviewers`, `showCreateReviewerPopup`, `CARD_CREATE`, and all ~15 associated fields (`createRepoConfig`, `createSourceBranchLabel`, `createTargetBranchCombo`, `createTitleField`, `createDescriptionArea`, `createReviewersPanel`, `createAddReviewerLink`, `createButton`, `createBackLabel`, `selectedReviewerUsernames`, `selectedReviewerDisplayNames`). Remove `add(createPanel, CARD_CREATE)` from `init`. Remove the `import com.workflow.orchestrator.core.settings.RepoConfig` import if it becomes unused.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed dead code. `grep -rn 'showCreateForm' pullrequest/src/` returns only the definition at line 466 — zero callers. The CLAUDE.md for :pullrequest explicitly states 'Single entry point to PR creation: PR tab's "+Create PR" button (via CreatePrLauncher.getInstance()?.launch(project, scope)). Build tab's entry was removed in the 2026-04-27 PrBar redesign.' `createRepoConfig` is already referenced at line 103 (class field) and used only within the dead `showCreateForm`/`buildCreatePanel`/`submitCreatePr` path. `buildCreatePanel` and `submitCreatePr` are private so they are also only reachable from `showCreateForm`. The ~300 lines inflate compile time and obscure the active code path. Cleanup is safe since there are no callers. Severity downgraded from Medium to Low: dead code is a maintainability issue, not a correctness/behavior issue.
recommendedAction: Delete `showCreateForm`, `buildCreatePanel`, `submitCreatePr`, `renderCreateReviewers`, `showCreateReviewerPopup`, the `CARD_CREATE` constant, and all ~15 associated fields listed in the finding. Remove `add(createPanel, CARD_CREATE)` from `init`. Verify `createRepoConfig` at line 103 (class field) becomes unused and remove it. Clean up any now-unused imports.

title: Dead AUTHOR/REVIEWER branches in fetchAllPages and effectively-unused username param
description: `fetchAllPages` is called from exactly one site (line 170) with `role = "ALL"`. The `"AUTHOR"` and `"REVIEWER"` arms of the `when` expression (lines 250–251) are therefore dead — they were the pre-dashboard per-repo loop replaced by `fetchDashboardPrs` but never removed. Consequently, the `username` parameter (line 243) is also never read at any live call site (always passed as `null` and only consumed in the dead arms).
evidence:
val results = fetchAllPages(client, projectKey, repoSlug, null, "ALL")  // line 170
// inside fetchAllPages:
"AUTHOR" -> client.getMyPullRequests(...)  // line 250, dead
"REVIEWER" -> client.getReviewingPullRequests(...)  // line 251, dead
suggestedFix: Collapse the `when` to just `client.getRepoPullRequests(projectKey, repoSlug, currentState, start, 25)` (the `else` branch). Remove the `username: String?` parameter from `fetchAllPages`. No behavior changes at any live call site.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `fetchAllPages` is invoked at exactly one site (PrListService.kt line 170) with `role = "ALL"` and `username = null`. The `"AUTHOR"` and `"REVIEWER"` branches at lines 250-251 are dead; they were the pre-dashboard per-repo role-filtered calls replaced by `fetchDashboardPrs` (which correctly uses `BitbucketBranchClient.getDashboardPullRequests`) as documented in the CLAUDE.md Bitbucket DC 9.4 audit changes section. The `username: String?` parameter is passed as `null` at the only call site and only consumed in those dead branches. Collapsing the `when` to just the `else` branch and removing the `username` parameter is a safe refactor with no behavioral change at any live call site. Severity is Low: the dead branches are not executable, they pose no correctness risk.
recommendedAction: Collapse the `when (role)` in `fetchAllPages` to a single unconditional call: `client.getRepoPullRequests(projectKey, repoSlug, currentState, start, 25)`. Remove the `username: String?` parameter from `fetchAllPages` and update the one call site at line 170 accordingly.

title: Unfinished public method resolveDefaultReviewersForBranch never called from outside its file
description: `CreatePrPrefetch.resolveDefaultReviewersForBranch` (line 106) is a `suspend fun` marked public on the companion object. Its KDoc says the dialog "re-runs this on target-change via `reloadDefaultReviewersForBranch`", but no such call exists in `CreatePrDialog` or anywhere else. It has zero callers outside its own file. The `@Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER")` on `project` (line 107) is an additional signal that the wiring was never completed.
suggestedFix: Either wire the call from `CreatePrDialog.onModuleChanged` / target-field `DocumentListener` (which the KDoc promises), or delete the method if the re-resolution feature is no longer planned. The method itself is correct; this is purely an unused-export cleanup.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `grep -rn 'resolveDefaultReviewersForBranch' pullrequest/src/` returns only three hits, all within `CreatePrPrefetch.kt` (definition + 2 log.warn lines inside the implementation). Zero callers from `CreatePrDialog`, `CreatePrLauncherImpl`, or any other file. The KDoc at line 95-105 describes wiring that was never implemented: 'the dialog re-runs this on target-change via reloadDefaultReviewersForBranch' — no such method exists in `CreatePrDialog`. The `@Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER")` on `project` at line 107 is an additional code signal that the wiring was planned but never completed. The method itself is functionally correct; it is unused public API. Low severity: no behavioral impact, purely a dangling export.
recommendedAction: Either wire the call from `CreatePrDialog`'s target-branch `DocumentListener` as the KDoc promises (the feature has clear value: re-resolving default reviewers when the target branch changes), or remove the method if the feature is no longer planned. If wired, also remove the `@Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER")` on `project` once it is used.

title: Force-dereference !! on ToolResult.data after isError guard in AiReviewTabPanel
description: Line 85 uses `commits.data!!` after `if (commits.isError) "" else commits.data!!…`. The same pattern was explicitly called out as a bug (`pullrequest:F-6 — forced !! NPE`) and fixed in `PrDetailPanel.kt` (lines 880–890) with a null-check instead. `ToolResult.data` is typed as `T` but is effectively nullable when `isError=true`, and the `isError=false` contract can be violated. The identical defect was already fixed elsewhere in the same module, so this is an oversight.
evidence:
val commits = service.getPullRequestCommits(prId, repoName = null)
if (commits.isError) "" else commits.data!!.firstOrNull()?.id.orEmpty()  // line 85
suggestedFix: Replace with `commits.data?.firstOrNull()?.id.orEmpty()`. Remove the `isError` guard entirely, since `?.` handles both the null case and the empty-success case uniformly.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed at AiReviewTabPanel.kt line 85: `commits.data!!` is used after `if (commits.isError) "" else commits.data!!…`. ToolResult.data is typed as `T?` (nullable) per ToolResult.kt line 26, and the KDoc says non-null when !isError is only a 'convention', not a type-level guarantee. The identical pattern was explicitly fixed in PrDetailPanel.kt lines 880-890 (the F-6 fix). However, the entire expression is wrapped in `runCatching { }` at line 83, so any NPE from `data!!` is caught and falls back to an empty `toHash` string — users see degraded (floating-anchor) AI review push, not a crash. The defect is real and inconsistent with the adjacent fix, but the `runCatching` eliminates the crash risk. The fix (`commits.data?.firstOrNull()?.id.orEmpty()` — dropping the isError guard) is safe and correct.
recommendedAction: Replace line 85 with `commits.data?.firstOrNull()?.id.orEmpty()` and remove the `isError` branch. Non-harmful cleanup that matches the F-6 fix already applied in PrDetailPanel.

title: Redundant popup.cancel() in showAddReviewerPopup — the callee already cancels before calling back
description: `showUserSearchPopup` calls `popup.cancel()` at line 1727 and then immediately invokes `onUserSelected(selected, popup)`. The `showAddReviewerPopup` lambda (the only caller of `showUserSearchPopup` that calls `popup.cancel()` inside the callback) duplicates this at line 1621, so the popup is cancelled twice. The second `cancel()` on a JBPopup that is already cancelled is benign but misleading.
suggestedFix: Remove the `popup.cancel()` call at line 1621 in `showAddReviewerPopup`. The closure still receives `popup` (needed as the `JBPopup` parameter in the lambda signature), but no action on it is needed.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: `showUserSearchPopup` calls `popup.cancel()` at line 1727 before invoking the `onUserSelected` callback, and `showAddReviewerPopup` (the sole callback wired at line 1619) calls `popup.cancel()` again at line 1621. The second cancel on an already-cancelled JBPopup is a no-op on the Swing side but is misleading. Removing line 1621's redundant `popup.cancel()` is safe — the popup parameter is still received in the lambda (the signature requires it) but nothing else in the lambda body uses `popup`. No tests or behavior changes.
recommendedAction: Remove the `popup.cancel()` call at PrDetailPanel.kt line 1621. Low-priority cleanup only.

title: Dead cachedVersion parameter in PrActionService.decline()
description: The `cachedVersion: Int = 0` parameter (line 196) is documented as "retained only for API compatibility" but is never read inside the function body — the implementation always fetches a fresh version via `fetchAndDecline`. The sole caller at `PrDetailPanel.kt:1263` passes no argument (uses the default). The parameter is pure dead weight that contradicts the project's `feedback_no_compat_shims.md` convention.
suggestedFix: Remove the `cachedVersion` parameter from `decline`. Update the KDoc to remove the outdated "retained only for API compatibility" sentence. The sole caller needs no change as it already passes no argument.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: `cachedVersion: Int = 0` at PrActionService.kt line 196 is documented as 'retained only for API compatibility' but the implementation always delegates to `fetchAndDecline` which fetches the version internally — `cachedVersion` is never referenced in the function body. The sole caller at PrDetailPanel.kt line 1263 passes no argument (uses the default). Removing the parameter and updating the KDoc is a clean non-breaking change — the caller uses no argument so no call-site change is needed.
recommendedAction: Remove the `cachedVersion: Int = 0` parameter from PrActionService.decline() and delete the outdated 'retained only for API compatibility' KDoc sentence.

title: PrListService.getClient() throws instead of returning null, risking uncaught exception in polling
description: `getClient()` (line 96–97) calls `error("Bitbucket URL not configured")` when the URL is blank, throwing `IllegalStateException`. `refresh()` at line 107 calls `getClient()` only after an early-exit check on `bitbucketUrl.isBlank()` (line 102), so the happy path is safe. However, the `SmartPoller` action lambda at line 54 calls `refresh()` directly, and any race where the URL becomes blank between the poller's scheduling and the actual call will propagate the exception up through the poller, potentially cancelling or corrupting the `SupervisorJob` child for that poll cycle. The CLAUDE.md threading contract states polling runs under `CoroutineScope+SupervisorJob` to isolate failures; an uncaught `IllegalStateException` escapes that isolation. All other PR services return `null` from their `getClient()` equivalents.
suggestedFix: Change `getClient()` to `return clientCache.get()` (nullable), and update `refresh()` to treat a null client as a no-op early-return with a log message (same pattern as `PrDetailService.getDetail`, `PrActionService.approve`, etc.). The `bitbucketUrl.isBlank()` pre-check in `refresh()` can be removed since `clientCache.get()` already returns null when the URL is blank.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: PrListService.getClient() at lines 96-97 throws `IllegalStateException` via `error(...)` when the URL is blank, while PrActionService.getClient() (line 40) returns null. The finding's claim that this would 'cancel or corrupt the SupervisorJob child' is overstated: SmartPoller's action lambda is wrapped in a try-catch (lines 55-67 of SmartPoller.kt) that catches all `Exception` types including `IllegalStateException`, logs a warning, and increases backoff — the job is NOT cancelled. The real race window (URL becomes blank between line 102's isBlank check and line 107's getClient call) is extremely narrow in practice. The issue is an inconsistency with the module's own pattern (every other service returns null) and does add unnecessary noise to logs, but it does not produce the crash/corruption described. Fixing it to match the nullable pattern is safe.
recommendedAction: Change PrListService.getClient() to return `clientCache.get()` (nullable) and update refresh() to return early on null client, matching the pattern used by PrActionService and the rest of the PR services. Severity should be treated as Low given SmartPoller catches the exception anyway.

title: PersistentStateComponent bitbucketUsername written from IO dispatcher, not EDT
description: The comment at lines 117–119 claims the mutation was moved to `Dispatchers.EDT`. The actual code at lines 119–121 wraps only the `connSettings.bitbucketUsername = result.data` assignment inside `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)`. However, `connSettings` is a direct reference to the mutable state object obtained at line 100 with `ConnectionSettings.getInstance().state`. The CLAUDE.md threading rule (D2 in the comment) requires that `PersistentStateComponent` state mutations happen on the EDT. While the assignment is dispatched to EDT, the surrounding code still reads `connSettings` from the IO thread (line 111: `connSettings.bitbucketUsername.takeIf {…}`). A concurrent EDT write (e.g., the user saving Settings) could produce a torn read on the IO thread. The fix is narrowly scoped but the underlying issue is that `connSettings` is a mutable state snapshot used across two threads.
suggestedFix: Read `ConnectionSettings.getInstance().state.bitbucketUsername` freshly on the EDT inside the `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)` block rather than holding a reference captured on IO. Better yet, use the canonical pattern: call `ConnectionSettings.getInstance().bitbucketUsername` (via the ApplicationService accessor) on EDT. The outer IO-thread check for a saved username (`connSettings.bitbucketUsername.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }`) should access the state via a snapshot read: `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT) { ConnectionSettings.getInstance().state.bitbucketUsername }` before the suspend network call.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: at PrListService.kt line 100, `connSettings` is captured as a direct mutable state reference on the IO dispatcher. Line 111 reads `connSettings.bitbucketUsername` on IO, while line 120 writes `connSettings.bitbucketUsername` on EDT. These are concurrent accesses to the same mutable object across two thread pools. However, the practical risk is narrow: this code path only executes once (on first refresh, before `cachedUsername` is set), the field is a simple String property on a POJO, and the worst case is a torn read (the IO thread sees a partially-written string) not data loss or corruption. The write-to-EDT fix was already partially applied (line 119 dispatch); the remaining gap is the IO-thread read. Fixing it by reading `ConnectionSettings.getInstance().state.bitbucketUsername` freshly on EDT before the network call is safe, but the claim of 'High' severity overstates a narrow single-pass race on a string field.
recommendedAction: Read the cached username check via `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT) { ConnectionSettings.getInstance().state.bitbucketUsername }` before the network call, matching the write dispatch already present at line 119. Low-priority threading hygiene.

title: PrDetailPanel's inline Create PR form (CARD_CREATE) is a dead code path — CreatePrLauncher EP is the sole entry point
description: The `CARD_CREATE` card and its entire `buildCreatePanel()` / `submitCreatePr()` / `showCreateForm()` implementation (lines 466–810, ~345 lines) exists alongside the dedicated `CreatePrDialog` + `CreatePrLauncherImpl` EP. The module's `CLAUDE.md` states: 'Single entry point to PR creation: PR tab's "+Create PR" button (via CreatePrLauncher.getInstance()?.launch(…))' and 'Build tab's entry was removed'. The `showCreateForm()` is public but is never called from within the module. The `CreatePrAction` inner class in `PrDashboardPanel` goes through `CreatePrLauncher.getInstance().launch(…)` exclusively. The inline form implements a subset of the dialog's features (no multi-ticket input, no AI description, no module selector, no Jira transition) and creates its own `BitbucketBranchClient` directly (line 714). This dead path adds maintenance burden and breaks the architecture convention of a single EP-driven entry point.
suggestedFix: Remove `CARD_CREATE`, `buildCreatePanel()`, `submitCreatePr()`, `showCreateForm()`, and all related fields (`createPanel`, `createSourceBranchLabel`, `createTargetBranchCombo`, `createTitleField`, `createDescriptionArea`, `createReviewersPanel`, `createAddReviewerLink`, `createButton`, `createBackLabel`, `selectedReviewerUsernames`, `selectedReviewerDisplayNames`, `createRepoConfig`). The `CreatePrLauncher` EP (invoked from `PrDashboardPanel.CreatePrAction`) is the correct and complete path.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed dead code. A global search for `showCreateForm` across the entire worktree returns exactly one result: its own definition at PrDetailPanel.kt:466. No caller exists anywhere in the codebase. `CARD_CREATE` is built in `init` (line 312-313) and shown only from within `showCreateForm` itself (line 500). `PrDashboardPanel.CreatePrAction` (lines 498-515) exclusively goes through `CreatePrLauncher.getInstance().launch(project, scope)` — the EP path. The inline form lacks multi-ticket input, AI description, Jira transitions, and the module selector that `CreatePrDialog` provides. Removing it is safe: the only reachable PR-creation path is the EP-driven dialog.
recommendedAction: Remove `CARD_CREATE`, `buildCreatePanel()`, `submitCreatePr()`, `showCreateForm()`, `renderCreateReviewers()`, `showCreateReviewerPopup()`, and all related private fields (`createPanel`, `createSourceBranchLabel`, `createTargetBranchCombo`, `createTitleField`, `createDescriptionArea`, `createReviewersPanel`, `createAddReviewerLink`, `createButton`, `createBackLabel`, `selectedReviewerUsernames`, `selectedReviewerDisplayNames`, `createRepoConfig`). Coordinate removal with any test that might reference `CARD_CREATE`.

title: PrListPanel empty-state label text does not follow the project's canonical empty-state convention
description: The project convention (CLAUDE.md UI section) requires empty states to read "No [entity] [state]." followed by an action link. `PrListPanel`'s empty label reads "No pull requests found. Click Refresh to update." (line 75) — the 'Click Refresh to update' text is a prose instruction embedded in the label rather than a separate clickable action link. No action link component is provided. The same panel also uses this label unconditionally for both 'not yet loaded' and 'loaded but empty' states, meaning the label text is misleading when polling is active but returned an empty result.
evidence: 75:    private val emptyLabel = JBLabel("No pull requests found. Click Refresh to update.").apply {
suggestedFix: Split into two states: (1) a 'No pull requests [state].' label (e.g., 'No pull requests open.') matching the convention, plus (2) a separate `JBLabel` with `foreground = LINK_COLOR` and a mouse listener that calls `PrListService.refresh()` as the action link. Pass the current state string into `updatePrs()` so the label reads 'No pull requests open.', 'No pull requests merged.', etc.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed at PrListPanel.kt:75. The label text is 'No pull requests found. Click Refresh to update.' — prose instruction embedded in the label with no separate clickable action link component. The project convention in CLAUDE.md mandates 'No [entity] [state].' plus an action link. Additionally, `showEmpty()` is called unconditionally for both 'not loaded' and 'loaded but empty' states, so the label text is always the same regardless of context. The fix is straightforward UI refactoring with no risk of breaking existing behavior.
recommendedAction: Replace the single static label with two components: a state-appropriate JBLabel (e.g. 'No pull requests open.') and a separate link-styled JBLabel that triggers PrListService.refresh(). Pass the current state string into showEmpty() so the label is contextual.
